data.title: OTHERS market value exceeds 27.4 billion US dollars, up 20% in three days
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to market data, the total market value (OTHERS) after excluding the top ten cryptocurrencies exceeded US$27.4 billion, a two-month high, with a three-day increase of 20%. In addition, OTHERS.D rose 8% in three days, and the market heat for small and medium-sized market value projects has rebounded significantly.

data.title: Europe Implements Capital Controls Amid Digital Euro Concerns
data.description: According to PANews, CitizenX CEO Alex Recouso announced on the X platform that Europe is enforcing capital controls. Spain will impose a fine of 3,000 euros for failing to report cash withdrawals exceeding 150 euros within 24 hours. In response, Tether CEO Paolo Ardoino commented that the atmosphere surrounding the digital euro is intensifying.

data.title: Bitcoin Volatility Declines to February Levels
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, recent data from Coinglass indicates that Bitcoin's volatility has decreased for two consecutive days, currently standing at 2.02%. This level of volatility has returned to what was observed at the end of February this year.

High volatility in Bitcoin is often associated with speculative trading and retail investors' fear of missing out (FOMO). A decline in volatility may suggest a reduction in short-term speculative activity, leading the market into a consolidation or 'calm' phase. Additionally, Bitcoin's price fluctuations are frequently linked to macroeconomic events such as inflation expectations, interest rate changes, or geopolitical risks. When these external factors stabilize, Bitcoin's volatility tends to decrease accordingly.

data.title: Bitcoin's Deflationary Nature Highlighted by Strategy's Holdings
data.description: According to PANews, Ki Young Ju, founder and CEO of CryptoQuant, recently stated on the X platform that Bitcoin exhibits deflationary characteristics. He noted that Strategy, formerly known as MicroStrategy, is acquiring Bitcoin at a pace surpassing the rate of Bitcoin mining. The company currently holds approximately 555,000 Bitcoins, which are in a non-liquid state with no plans for sale. This situation allows Strategy's Bitcoin holdings to achieve an annual deflation rate of -2.23%. Other stable institutional holders may experience even higher deflation rates with their Bitcoin holdings.

data.title: XT will be available for trading on UDS (Undeads Games)
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News XT is about to launch UDS (Undeads Games) and open UDS/USDT trading pairs in the Innovation Zone (Metaverse).
Undeads Games is a multi-chain metaverse game ecosystem that includes Web2/Web3 games based on Ethereum and TON blockchains. After three and a half years of development, the flagship product Undeads game was launched exclusively to the $UDS community in closed beta mode on May 31, and the official version is scheduled to be released in September 2025. In addition, the ecosystem also offers the Undeads Rush game, as well as the survival game Undeads Fighters, which will be launched in the form of a Telegram applet in July 2025.

data.title: Bitcoin Privacy Wallet Developers Face Legal Charges in the U.S.
data.description: According to PANews, developers of the Bitcoin privacy wallet Samourai Wallet, Keonne Rodriguez and William Lonergan Hill, are facing charges of money laundering and operating an unlicensed money transmission business. Despite the defense's argument that the U.S. Financial Crimes Enforcement Network (FinCEN) had indicated the wallet might not meet the definition of a money transmitter under the Bank Secrecy Act, the prosecution has refused to drop the charges. They also denied any "Brady violation," which refers to the non-disclosure of evidence favorable to the defendant. The prosecution maintains that FinCEN's opinion is unrelated to the more serious money laundering charges. The case is expected to proceed as planned, with a trial anticipated within the next seven months.

data.title: The whale transferred 7,665 ETH and 2.17 million ENA to Binance, with a loss of ETH of $8.84 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News, according to OnchainLens monitoring, the address unpledged the last 7,665 ETH (about 18.3 million US dollars) from StakeStone today and transferred it to Binance. The current investment loss is about 8.84 million US dollars.
The address had withdrawn 15,332.7 ETH (about 51.7 million US dollars) from Binance 4 months ago. In addition, the address also unpledged 2.17 million ENA (about 821,000 US dollars) and transferred it to Binance.

data.title: CryptoQuant CEO: Strategy purchase speed is faster than BTC mining, and has achieved an annualized deflation advantage of 2.23%
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News CryptoQuant founder and CEO Ki Young Ju posted on the X platform that Bitcoin has deflationary properties, and Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) is currently buying more Bitcoin than it can mine. The company holds about 555,000 Bitcoins, which are illiquid and have no plans to sell.
He pointed out that this means that Strategy's BTC holdings have an annual deflation rate of -2.23%, while other long-term institutional investors may enjoy higher deflationary advantages.

data.title: Samourai Wallet case prosecutors deny violating the Brady Rule
data.description:
Prosecutors in the Samourai Wallet case have denied violating the Brady rule. According to a letter sent to the Southern District of New York (SDNY) filed today, the prosecutors mentioned that they did not withhold exculpatory evidence. In the letter, the prosecutors also urged the judge to deny any request by Samourai for a late […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/samourai-wallet-case-prosecutors-deny-violating-the-brady-rule/

data.title: The author of "Rich Dad Poor Dad" said that people should stay away from "fake currencies" and turn to BTC, gold and silver
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Robert Kiyosaki, author of Rich Dad Poor Dad, once again called on Americans to abandon "fake money" and turn to assets such as Bitcoin, gold and silver. He cited the views of former Congressman Ron Paul, criticizing the central bank's interest rate setting as "price manipulation", which brought about a dishonest monetary system, false data and harmful leadership, and was eroding people's wealth.
Kiyosaki has long advocated the use of decentralized assets to hedge against inflation risks, and has predicted that Bitcoin will exceed $1 million by 2035. (Cointelegraph)
